prompt.content:
There are set of treatments known as "Concept" with a unique 'Concept Codes'; Further, the client has provided the bill details mapped to 'Concept Codes'. Also hospitals have internal 'concept IDs' mapped uniquely with codes. The codes are categorised into Code family, based on which they are taxed. The starting letter of the code determines the family as detailed in the table below: 
Family Number | Concept Code Family| Tax %
F1 | A to F| 10.00%
F2 | G to L| 12.50%
F3 | M to S| 13.00%
F4 | T to Z| 13.50%
Task -  For each of the Concept Code Family listed above, calculate and return the average billed amount (in USD) of the concept codes falling in that category. Also, return the count of times they were billed last month for each Concept Code Family.
Assumptions-
1. In each bill, account for a code once. Ignore any duplicate codes in the billing amount. 
2. Total amount in the bill sheet is post-tax.
3. The ratio between the average base rates of F1 and F2 is 1:2 and F3 and F4 is 1:3
Report all outcomes as a message in the console. Round the final average billed amount to the nearest 2 decimal places and count the number of times billed to the nearest whole number.
